semantic.role: Infrastructure utility module providing text truncation and compaction functions for prompt context budget enforcement.
responsibilities: Trim a string to a maximum number of lines, appending truncation indicator.
Trim a string to a maximum number of characters, preserving whole lines up to the limit.
Split concatenated rule data into individual rule blocks using a delimiter pattern.
Trim a concatenated rule string to a character limit, keeping complete rule blocks.
Trim sectioned context (separated by double newlines) to a character limit, preserving whole sections.
Compact a FileSkeleton object into a budget-aware string representation for prompt inclusion.
Trim the previous semantic context object to a maximum number of responsibility entries.
outOfScope: Any I/O operations such as file reading or network calls.
Any interaction with LLM providers or prompt sending.
Any UI rendering or CLI output formatting.
Any domain-specific validation or business rule enforcement.
invariants: All exported functions are pure side-effect-free transformations of their inputs.
The module does not depend on any configuration or mutable state.
Budget parameters are always respected – trimming never exceeds the specified maximum.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide stable, composable trimming utilities that allow prompt construction to respect token/character budgets without leaking truncation logic into higher layers.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Finalize the prompt context trimming system for the v1.0.0 release, ensuring all context types (lines, characters, rule blocks, sections, file skeletons, previous semantic) have dedicated budget-aware truncation functions.

publicSurface.description: Truncates a string to a specified number of lines, appending '...' if truncated. Used to cap multi-line text in prompts.
Truncates a string to a maximum number of characters while keeping whole lines. Used for character-based budget enforcement.
Splits a concatenated string of rule definitions into an array of individual rule blocks, based on the '[Rule: ' delimiter.
Truncates concatenated rule definitions to a character limit, preserving complete rule blocks (not splitting mid-rule).
Truncates context data separated by two newlines to a maximum character count, keeping whole sections intact.
Converts a FileSkeleton and prompt budgets into a compact string representation suitable for inclusion in a prompt, respecting budget limits.
Truncates the PreviousSemanticContext object to a maximum number of responsibility entries, reducing context size for large histories.

outOfScope: Any I/O operations such as file reading or network calls.
Any interaction with LLM providers or prompt sending.
Any UI rendering or CLI output formatting.
Any domain-specific validation or business rule enforcement.

exports.name: trimPreviousSemantic
exports.signature:
trimPreviousSemantic(
  previousSemantic: PreviousSemanticContext | undefined,
  maxResponsibilities: number,
)
